name: Ireland
description: The European headquarters for Google, Apple, and Facebook. Ireland offers an English-speaking environment with a booming tech and pharma sector, making it ideal for career-focused students.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: It is highly recommended that the tuition fee should not be sponsored only through the education loan, which means the sponsor should have 6 months old liquid funds in their account apart from education loan.
tuitionFees.postgraduate: Evidence that the student has immediate access to Tuition Fees + Living expenses for all the years. €12,000 is the estimated cost of living in Ireland as per Irish Embassy for a student for one academic year. The cost of total duration of study and stay needs to be shown in liquid funds such as Education Loan, Bank Balances and Fixed Deposits.
tuitionFees.phd: €5,000–€15,000 per year; many include research bursaries

scholarships.name: Scholarships

requirements: Valid passport (valid for the full duration of study)
Letter of acceptance from an Irish institution
Proof of financial means (€7,000+ for the first year)
Private medical insurance (if not covered by an employer)
Academic transcripts and English proficiency certificates
Evidence of payment of first year tuition fees

workPermit.duringStudy: Up to 20 hours/week during term; 40 hours during summer
workPermit.postStudy: 1–2 years Stay Back Option (SBOT): 2 years for degree graduates, 1 year for others
workPermit.notes: Ireland is the only English-speaking EU country — ideal for EU career access

languageRequirements.notes: English language proficiency required for all programs

faqs.question: Is it easy to get a student visa for Ireland?
faqs.answer: With proper documentation and financial proof, obtaining a student visa for Ireland is straightforward. MVR Consultants can guide you through the entire process.

faqs.question: What are the tuition fees in Ireland?
faqs.answer: Tuition fees vary by university and program. Contact our counselors for program-specific fee structures.

faqs.question: Can I work while studying?
faqs.answer: Most countries allow international students to work part-time during term time. Specific hours and conditions depend on the country's immigration rules.

faqs.question: Are there scholarships available for Indian students?
faqs.answer: Yes, many universities and governments offer merit-based and need-based scholarships for international students. Ask our counselors for scholarship matching.
